The Alphabet

The Greek alphabet consists of 24 distinct graphemes. It is the foundation of Western writing. Mastery of these symbols is the first step to literacy.

Letter Name Sound Example
Α α Alpha [a] hat
Β β Beta [v] vase
Γ γ Gamma [γ] rendez-vous
Δ δ Delta [δ] than
Ε ε Epsilon [e] pen
Ζ ζ Zeta [z] zoo
Η η Eta [i] tip
Θ θ Theta [θ] thin
Ι ι Iota [i] tip
Κ κ Kappa [k] book
Λ λ Lambda [l] like
Μ μ Mu [m] make
Ν ν Nu [n] noise
Ξ ξ Xi [ks] ox
Ο ο Omicron [o] top
Π π Pi [p] stop
Ρ ρ Rho [r] Roberto
Σ σ/ς Sigma [s] see
Τ τ Tau [t] spot
Υ υ Upsilon [i] tip
Φ φ Phi [f] fish
Χ χ Chi [h] loch
Ψ ψ Psi [ps] stops
Ω ω Omega [o] top

Orthographic Notes

  • Case Sensitivity: Proper nouns and sentence starters are capitalized.
  • Question Mark: Greek uses a semicolon (;) as a question mark.
  • Sigma: Use σ normally, but ς at the end of a word.
